---

reference_answer: The picture in this context is volatile rather than one clear driver: TCS shares rallied 4% on a steady Q1 FY27 show with management expecting demand to resume in Q2 (Jul 2026 headline), but that follows a 6-year-low, 8-9% single-day crash in early June 2026 -- its worst single-day fall since the Covid-era. Before that, it hit a 52-week low amid a broader IT sector selloff (Feb 2026), while earlier in the year it had gained on being named an ICICI Securities top IT pick (Mar 2026) and risen on strong financials (Nov 2025 headline). This context doesn't include specific revenue/profit figures or named deal wins.
